description: We want to create a new newsletter view that uses a card layout, so three cards across. We would take the email newsletters that come in and we would show them as a card. The card would contain, ideally, a hero image and some quick links. We want to make this more like a news page and make it easy for folks to view their newsletter content in a more delightful way. Can you think about how you would create a new view on the inbox page that would be more delightful, more like a Pinterest style?
title: Create newsletter card grid view

descriptionHistory.description: We want to create a new newsletter view that uses a card layout, so three cards across. We would take the email newsletters that come in and we would show them as a card. The card would contain, ideally, a hero image and some quick links. We want to make this more like a news page and make it easy for folks to view their newsletter content in a more delightful way. Can you think about how you would create a new view on the inbox page that would be more delightful, more like a Pinterest style?

summary:
## Summary: Pinterest-style Newsletter Card View

### Changes Implemented
- **New API endpoint** (`POST /api/emails/newsletter-cards`) that takes email IDs and extracts hero images and quick links from newsletter HTML using regex-based parsing. Includes intelligent filtering to skip tracking pixels, tiny icons, and irrelevant links (unsubscribe, privacy policy, etc.)
- **New `NewsletterCardView` component** with a Pinterest-style masonry layout using CSS columns (1 column mobile, 2 tablet, 3 desktop). Each card shows:
  - Hero image extracted from the newsletter (with pastel initial fallback when no image found)
  - Sender avatar with color-coded initial
  - Email subject and preview text (with line clamping)
  - Up to 3 quick links extracted from the newsletter content
  - Unread indicator, unsubscribe link, mark-done action, and open-full link
  - Hover effects with shadow and border highlighting
- **View mode toggle** in the inbox toolbar (both desktop and mobile):
  - Desktop: Segmented button group (list icon / cards icon) next to the Labels button
  - Mobile: Single toggle button in the mobile toolbar
- **Smart layout behavior**: When in card view mode, the preview pane and resize handle are hidden, giving the card grid full width
- Card view works with all existing filters (folder, account, label, smart label, search)

### Files Modified
- `app/(app)/inbox/page.tsx` — Added import, viewMode state, toggle buttons (desktop + mobile), conditional rendering for card vs list view, hide preview pane in card mode

### Files Created
- `app/api/emails/newsletter-cards/route.ts` — API endpoint to extract hero images and links from email HTML
- `components/email/NewsletterCardView.tsx` — Pinterest-style masonry card layout component

### Notes for Developer
- The card view works with all emails, not just those flagged `is_newsletter`. You can combine it with the existing "Newsletter" label filter to show only newsletters in card form
- Hero image extraction uses regex (no DOM parser dependency) to keep the server-side lightweight. It filters out tracking pixels, spacer GIFs, favicons, and sub-200px images
- Quick links are capped at 5 per card (3 shown), with deduplication and filtering of non-content links
- Card data is fetched lazily — only when emails appear that haven't been parsed yet
- The `line-clamp-2` utility requires Tailwind's line-clamp plugin (already included in modern Tailwind)
- Failed hero images gracefully fall back to the sender initial display
